assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: The conventions.md 'Source Files Analyzed' section explicitly lists all four files: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, and common/src/error.rs, along with 8 additional sibling files. The plan.md also references the patterns from these files throughout (e.g., 'following the pattern in advisory/endpoints/get.rs' in File 2, 'AdvisoryService' methods in File 4, 'AppError' from error.rs in File 2).

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan lists exactly 3 files to create (severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint, advisory_summary.rs tests) and 3 files to modify (advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs). All 6 files match the task's specified scope. The plan explicitly notes 'server/src/main.rs — no changes needed'.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's 'Files to Create' table lists: (1) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs for the SeveritySummary struct, (2) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs for the GET handler, and (3) tests/api/advisory_summary.rs for integration tests.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's 'Files to Modify' table lists: (4) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs, (5) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and (6) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs — all three required files.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: The commit message is 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' which follows Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope 'advisory', and description. The footer includes 'Refs: TC-9201' as a ticket reference.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: The commit command in the plan explicitly includes: --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code'

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan includes a 'Branch' section with 'TC-9201/add-advisory-severity-aggregation' which contains the Jira issue ID TC-9201 as the branch name prefix.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: The conventions.md identifies both patterns: Section 1 'Module Structure' describes the model/ + service/ + endpoints/ three-layer pattern, and Section 5 'Error Handling' describes 'Result<T, AppError>' with '.context() wrapping on every ? propagation'. Additionally identifies 8 more patterns (endpoint handler, route registration, service method, model/struct, testing, naming, import style, database query).
